note: Wallet & backup security documentation. New canonical markdown at docs/wallet-and-backup-security.md (single source of truth, EN + ES) covering: what is on-chain vs local, why private keys cannot be auto-stored, the two-layer backup strategy (12-word seed on paper + encrypted wallet JSON), recovery commands verified against sost-cli (newwallet / hd create / hd restore / getnewaddress / listaddresses / info), the 1000-block coinbase maturity rule with the IMMATURE / SPENDABLE display the wallet shows, and an honest description of why running the same address on two miners does not double the hashrate (overlapping templates / nonce space, not '95% wasted'). New website page sost-wallet-security.html mirrors the doc with the site theme; sost-help.html now links to it as a primary CTA. Web wallet seed-phrase generation flow gained a two-gate confirmation: a red callout panel hides the 12 words until the user clicks 'I understand — reveal seed phrase', and the 'Continue to Dashboard' button stays disabled until the user explicitly checks 'I have written all 12 words on physical paper, in order, and verified them'. Defence-in-depth: confirmSeedSaved also re-checks the box before navigating, so a programmatic click cannot skip the gate. NO consensus, NO node, NO miner, NO RPC schema, NO transaction format. Force-reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
